$comment: c112 (D-FFI-PE-IMPORT-THUNK): the address-taken-import RUN witness. A static-init array holds the ADDRESS of an imported function (`puts`) — sqlite's os_win.c aSyscall[] shape — and main CALLS it indirectly (a `volatile` load so neither the baseline nor the release arm can fold the indirect call into a direct `puts(...)`, keeping the address-taken path live at runtime). Every hosted format must produce a CALLABLE address for an address-taken import: ELF via a PLT stub, Mach-O via a __stubs entry, and now PE via a synthesized `jmp *[IAT slot]` import THUNK (externCallDispatch=direct-plt; symbolVa[extern]=thunk VA, a .text code address). A correct run prints the line and exits 42. RED-on-disable: flip pe64-x86_64-windows-exec's `externCallDispatch` back to `indirect-slot` (or drop the pe.cpp thunk emission) -> the extern's address resolves to the raw .idata IAT data slot -> `call *ptr` executes data as code -> 0xC0000005 (no exit 42). The elf/arm64 legs already ran this construct green (sqlite on Linux, c91/c93), so all three legs prove the same source runs everywhere.
